channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18687] Separate userspace/compat code from kernel code
item.description:
The Lustre tree should maintain a clear distinction between userspace code, compat code, and kernel code.

 

https://wiki.lustre.org/Upstream_contributing
item.summary: Separate userspace/compat code from kernel code

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64329
Subject: LU-18687 build: eliminate automake
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 97684cc562ac5681a4936d6c242ada108bb91a8f

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64327
Subject: LU-18687 build: refactor the top-level Makefile
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 7b6e626a9ab66fbd12bebab952e7fe271a1e5178

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59834/
Subject: LU-18687 compat: move account_page_dirtied() import to symbols.c
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: c8f09fd352d43333efca210ca347b46fd60eb179

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64064
Subject: LU-18687 debug: restore option to disable cdebug
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 1ee08fc2e2681e72d727e8bafbb40fce50fbd95a

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64049
Subject: LU-18687 debug: cleanly split lustre_compat and debug
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 0ddb0efd02954c8b9304ed263882ad0257ffc6cf

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64027
Subject: LU-18687 rpm: libcfs/ include is no longer in libcfs/
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: ac52eedd698488968720d26bea89e82321b7619b
this happens if MEMCG is disabled in the kernel, I've created LU-19871 to fix this.

with the patch landed I can't build Lustre on linux-5.14.0-570.62.1.el9_6:

make[3]: Entering directory '/home/lustre/linux-5.14.0-570.62.1.el9_6'
  CC [M]  /home/lustre/master-mine/libcfs/libcfs/../../lustre_compat/symbols.o
  CC [M]  /home/lustre/master-mine/libcfs/libcfs/../../lustre_compat/misc.o
/home/lustre/master-mine/libcfs/libcfs/../../lustre_compat/symbols.c:91:6: error: redefinition of 'folio_memcg_lock'
   91 | void folio_memcg_lock(struct folio *folio)
      |      ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
In file included from /home/lustre/master-mine/libcfs/libcfs/../../lustre_compat/symbols.c:13:
./include/linux/memcontrol.h:1530:20: note: previous definition of 'folio_memcg_lock' with type 'void(struct folio *)'
 1530 | static inline void folio_memcg_lock(struct folio *folio)
      |                    ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
/home/lustre/master-mine/libcfs/libcfs/../../lustre_compat/symbols.c:99:6: error: redefinition of 'folio_memcg_unlock'
   99 | void folio_memcg_unlock(struct folio *folio)
      |      ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
In file included from /home/lustre/master-mine/libcfs/libcfs/../../lustre_compat/symbols.c:13:
./include/linux/memcontrol.h:1534:20: note: previous definition of 'folio_memcg_unlock' with type 'void(struct folio *)'
 1534 | static inline void folio_memcg_unlock(struct folio *folio)
      |                    ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
cc1: note: unrecognized command-line option '-Wno-gnu' may have been intended to silence earlier diagnostics
cc1: note: unrecognized command-line option '-Wno-gnu' may have been intended to silence earlier diagnostics

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/59835/
Subject: LU-18687 compat: move folio_memcg_lock/unlock() import to symbols.c
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 59080e87c74779ce2dec4145a02f9a885f8aa976

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63070/

Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 6260c420171de012da4a1cdf21f6dce392ba69f6

Commit: e72f51d9915c9ac704b26c56e3126b131ef03751
lustre/utils and lustre/tests probably make sense. I'm worried the symlinks will break the build/packaging in odd ways. So I'll have to try it out and see what happens.

To minimize disruption, I'm wondering if it makes sense to keep lustre/utils, lustre/tests, lustre/doc, lustre/compat, and add symlinks from the relocated lustre/* directories to the new locarions under fs/lustre, and from lnet to net/lnet, and libcfs to net/libcfs.
